Handle.HandleStory: Saw the name on an Orb album
Always been a Star Wars fan, so...
I was a huge fan of the Dragonlance books.

Scener.Trivia:
Pre-scene, Raistlin was a prolific POKEster (ZZAP!, Commodore User, CCI, What Poke and many more).
Joined the demo scene in 1988 with his brother, Kreator (<scener id=7835>), creating SCC (Silsden Cracking Crew) .. soon after joined Pyrotech and, later, co-formed Excess.
Joined Genesis Project (G*P) - which became his proper "home" - after short stints in a few other groups.
Quit the scene in 1991, after Delirious 10, to focus on University studies.
27 years later, in 2018, he rejoined G*P and released Delirious 11 at Gubbdata 2018 - which took 2nd place. Later that year, with just a 3-month development window, released X Marks the Spot at X2018 (which took 4th place).
Raistlin has been in the games industry since 1994 and has worked on many games - Destruction Derby, the Test Drive series, LA Rush, Wheelman, Infinity Blade, the Gears of War series, Fortnite, Lawbreakers, Conan Exiles, Sea of Thieves and more... as well as working on Unreal Engine.
Moved to Thailand in 2021.
Created several C64 related websites:-
https://c64demo.com : a coding blog https://c64gfx.com : a site collecting C64 artworks https://sidquake.c64demo.com : a tool for easy packaging of SIDs as executables with visualiser, scroller etc https://dirquake.c64demo.com : a tool - mostly for C64GFX.com - to quickly grab screenshots of C64 DIR art

Handle.HandleStory: Originally created as an alter-ego when submitting POKEs to Zzap!64.
I made a typo once I coded some junk and I liked the "handle" and 6581 was added later on, since it responds the SID soundchip. ...known to like sisters :)

Scener.Trivia:
Most recognized for my HQ SID Recordings. Recorded from real hardware. http://www.youtube.com/demoscenes
Involved in "Preservation", to preserve the demoscene and unreleased releases for the future generation of data-sceners, basically to pass that time-capsule and legacy. Where other quiet, that is where I begin to tackle the hard-cases of floppies and tapes. To bring out as much data as possible! "The challenge is always accepted"
http://sidpreservation.6581.org -Read about all the work and what is going on-
Fulltime musician: http://www.soundcloud.com/dees-productions

Handle.HandleStory: For a time I only used xiny, as came up from a typo I made while coding but the "nick" had a nice touch so I dropped my old nick and started to use this one instead and the numbers 6581, well they are from the SID chip, since I am a musican. End of story :D

UserComment.Text: Such a nice tune. When I heard this and yeah I didn't see who made it. I knew at once it HAS to be the Y and Y <3 Really fresh and totally coated in sugar!

Scener.Trivia:
Handle change from Mc Moneysack to Steel mentioned in <release id=212933>.
Co-Founder of Success
Left the scene in 1993 and became a renowned graffiti artist.
Came back to the scene in 2016.

UserComment.Text: Such a pleasure Meeting you in Person. Awesome Tune i really think this is heavily underrated in the compo results. I am Happy you two Guys Bless us more with That Type of sids i really Love.

Scener.Trivia:
*** All of my tunes are SID8580 only. If they do anything on a SID6581 chip, it's a mere coincidence. ***
As a C64 composer I was born in 1989, died in 1994 and re-born in 2009 because of C64 remix album "rakBIT".
For everything, big thanks to all my friends. You know who you are and I do, too!
___Albums and side projects___
- NECROLEPSY (2025): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/necrolepsy-deluxe-edition
- SCOPEKILLERS compilation (2022): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/scopekillers-compilation
- DESTRONNAUTH (2019): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/destronnauth
- HYPERGAME (2017): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/hypergame
- AENTHERA (2016): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/aenthera
- Bleep and Destroy (2014) by StrayWorx: https://strayboom.bandcamp.com/album/bleep-and-destroy
___C64 music collections___
- Darth SIDe: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/darth-side
- V8bit: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/v8bit
- Zeros as Ones: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/zeros-as-ones
- Voyage Eclectique: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/voyage-eclectique
___SID remix releases___
- Re:ReLive (2024) by SIDRIP Alliance: https://sidrip.bandcamp.com/album/re-relive
- ReLive (2015) by SIDRIP Alliance: https://sidrip.bandcamp.com/releases
- Johannes Bjerregaard tribute album: https://johannesbjerregaard.bandcamp.com/album/jb
- rakBIT by NP: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/rakbit
- Dark Flow by NP: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/dark-flow-the-expanding-ep
***
Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/user/NecroPolo8580/videos
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/NecroPolo-138733566144931/?ref=tn_tnmn
Remix64: http://www.remix64.com/member/necropolo/
BandCamp: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/music

Handle.HandleStory: An another old handle that I used when I started to make SIDs for games. None became released and some time after the developer's house burned down during the development of Geister in 1994 with all the sources I stopped making C64 music.
My very first scene handle that brought me an ample of finished tunes that I made for stuff that folks did not finish at the end in the early '90s.

UserComment.Text:
Great pace, chugging forward like a train with melodies to whistle along. The small chaos factor is a plus in my opinion, not a minus.
Youth, mate, it's been great to meet you again. I realise only after the party that at the end I talked with folks I'm really fond of much less than I intended too due to energy mismanagement but I'll try better next time.
And yeah Stinsen, you were missed.

Great work, partner-in-last-minute-party-music-composing :) I’ve had that initial melody stuck on my mind since the weekend. As mentioned before, sure, maybe a bit many different parts but still it works really well all in all. And the sound design (specifically the snare) is so good. Oh and those 16th-note-runs at 0:50 etc are lovely!
@Randall: Sorry, fault on my side! Reading comprehension is obviously not one of my fortes! :D @Youth: Yeah, totally missed not being there. I hope to rectify that next time...

Handle.HandleStory: generic enough to match Jammer and Sidder, my only groupmates at the time :)

Scener.Trivia: to further inflate my sack of nicks, i'm also (un)known as glebogryzarka and afrojax elsewhere

Handle.HandleStory: I named myself after one of my favourite songs (by Bronski Beat ofc).
chuj = obscene description of penis in polish, but with a much broader meaning than just that, frequently used as an insult. -inho, because i hail from minas gerais ;)
Valerie Solanas meets C64 coverbands, chaos ensues
a miserable wordplay on my favourite female singer's name. also a bit outdated political statement.

UserComment.Text: @Stinsen - apparently i couldn't express myself as intended :D this IS my favourite, but contrary to some other entries i loved, it sounds more - how to put it - universal?
Chaotic? A bit. But it's a damn good track. It's always a pleasure to meet and talk to you, dear Youth.

Thanks guys, it was great getting to talk to my fellow musicians in person. @Stinsen I missed you, maybe next time? You're absolutely right about the chaos, this tune was lying around for ages, not knowing how to get it into shape. Finally I decided to spend the friday at the party to squeeze it into three minutes and just ride the rollercoaster.

I see where the title is coming from and why it's maybe not 100% Randall compatible. ;D Love the main bass + percussions here though and the recognizable (?) riffs, as a whole maybe a bit chaotic though. :D
Very nice sounds and pleasing composition.

Handle.HandleStory: Name of the main character of the anime "SF Saiyuki Starzinger" by Leiji Matsumoto. At least here in italy, since in japanese is written Jan Kugo. I've chosen the handle "IAN" to sign my high scores on arcade games ^_^, then changed to Ian7102 in the late 80's, and finally iAN CooG from 1999 or so.

UserComment.Text: D/V: because someone should stop uploading made up RSID files on deepsid if he doesn't know how to properly rip and make sid files.
#16? almost all my personal favourites landed in the bottom half, which doesn't surprise me anymore - but this one seems to have so many virtues beyond catering to my taste :(

Handle.HandleStory: My first nickname was Short-Circuit and I did use this one in the demo group 'Future Technologics 2009'. My second handle was G-Force. But when the graphics card 'Nvidia GeForce' came out, i decided to search a new handle and this was G-Fellow. The G and F represent my first and family name.

Scener.Trivia:
I had contact with the C64 Scene since 1984 and I was in the demo group 'Future Technologics 2009' from 1987-1991 as swapper. In 1988 I did start to compose music, first in the Future Composer. After this I used Voicetracker. I did teach myself composing learning by doing. I play not any music instrument, that I did not learn any music instrument can sometimes be a advantage but sometimes also a pain.
In 1991 I stopped with the C64 and I moved to the PC. I made quite alot tunes on the PC with the Scream Tracker, some Scream Tracker tunes was uploaded on some Mailboxes.
1998 I found my C64 in a hidden place and I made some tunes for my own pleasure in the Soundbooster from Midas. Between 1998 - 2003 I made some PC-Songs with a other music-editor.
Around 2003 I found in the net some Scene pages and finally in 2004 I rejoined the C64 Scene as Musician. I was very happy that the JCH Editor was now free available and I must say that JCH was with his JCH Editor miles before its time (maybe Time-Machine).

UserComment.Text: I thought you rank higher, great tune! Keep em coming!

Handle.HandleStory:
I got this name/handle when I started to switch from Anormalperson to D/V
At the end of 2023, I started thinking of a cool name to change from Anormalperson Then I thought, "Hey, if I take my full name's initials and add a slash in between (I thought it was cool at the time), then it will become D/V." Long story short, I made a YouTube video telling everyone that I changed from Anormalperson to D/V, and here I am today on CSDb and a member of Hokuto Force (as of September 14th, 2025)

Scener.Trivia: D/V is actually short for Daniel Veronski (D/V's full name)

Handle.HandleStory: I used this name for quite a while before I changed to D/V

UserComment.Text:
Wait, how the hell is it 29x speed on DeepSID
Good coop btw :)
Woops thanks Raistlin, had a bad connection @ X
Download missing... Youth, did you forget to upload?
